name: Week 11 KB Conditioning — Skill Drill (Spotlight: Windmill Mechanics)
description: Thursday is the lightest day of the program. Recovery flank between Wed pull and Fri legs. **Spotlight rotates to windmill mechanics** — the bent press's sibling lift, same lateral-hinge family. Drill the long-spine + hip-flexor-of-back-leg cue at 18-20lb. Tuesday's bent press leverage cue should now feel automatic; the windmill borrows the same hinge and adds the deeper hamstring-and-back-leg-quad reach. 20 min, zero leg loading, no swings, no C&P, no goblet squats.

dayOverview.theme: Thursday recovery + skill drilling. Spotlight: windmill — the bent press's sibling. Same lateral-hinge family, deeper reach into the back leg.

spotlights.why: Windmill and bent press share the lateral-hinge family. Bent press got the spotlight in W10 Thursday; W11 Thursday hands the spotlight to windmill so the family completes. The windmill goes deeper into the back leg's hip flexor and the hamstrings — drill that reach at light load (18-20lb), spine-long, eye-on-bell.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 bent press · W11 windmill mechanics (current) · W12 TGU left-side checkpoints · W13 cycle back to bent press. Thursday is always lighter than Tuesday's spotlight.
watchpoints: **Zero leg loading.** No swings, no C&P, no goblet squats, no lunges. Legs day is tomorrow.
If anything feels heavy, the load is wrong. Pull weight or skip the block.
Windmill stretch should land in the **hip flexor of the back leg + hamstrings**, NOT the lower back. Lower-back stretch = side-bending, not hinging.

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 15-20lb. Slow circles, both directions. Shoulder loosener.

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Heels tucked, mini-band overhead, hands wider than shoulders. **Sustained tension.** Or use the W10D2 alt: blue rope band gripped in hands.
Pull band down behind head — **elbows drive to ribs**, scaps depress.
Band wraps the upper back. **Press out to T** — fires rear delts and mid-traps.
Band at sternum, **pull apart, scaps retract**, ribs down.
Sweep arms further back. **Don't overarch** the lower back.
15s each side. Half-windmill with band — **primes today's windmill spotlight directly.**
Both arms in V/Y overhead. **Hold and breathe** — lower traps fire here.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Sustained tension, no rest between positions. Diagonal reach is today's direct prime for the windmill spotlight.

sets.name: OTD Flow (Light Groove)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 20lb. Light and smooth.
- Bell starts on the floor between feet
- Hips drive the lift, not the arm
- Catch in rack with knees slightly bent
Goblet/rack position. Sit tall, full depth, no bouncing.
Stride out wide, sit into the working leg, keep the other leg long. Mobility, not strength.
Tuesday's spotlight cue (leverage — body goes down, bell barely moves). Light groove here.
**Control on the way down** — bell to floor with the same cleanness as up.

sets.notes: 20lb, 2 reps each side x 2 rounds. Should feel effortless throughout.

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 18-20lb (light to keep cues clean — NOT 25). 2 rounds.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Bell locked overhead, EYES TRACK THE BELL** — same as bent press. Lose the eyes, lose the path.
- **Hip hinge AWAY from bell side** — this is the windmill's bent-press inheritance. Push the bell-side hip OUT.
- **LONG SPINE** — the stretch should land in the **hip flexor of the back leg + hamstrings**, NOT the lower back. If you feel it in the lumbar, you're side-bending — re-set, hinge instead.
- **Reach the free hand toward the INSIDE of the front foot** — that's the line of pull. Outside foot = wrong angle.
- **Bell stays vertical** the entire time — if it drifts inward, the arm is collapsing
- **Slow descent, brief pause at the bottom, controlled return** — speed kills the groove

**Reflection:** does the stretch land in the back leg's hip flexor (correct), or the lower back (wrong)? The windmill is a hip-and-back-leg lift; the lower back should feel NEUTRAL throughout. If it doesn't, drop to 15lb or shorten the descent.

**LOAD:** 18-20lb. Light groove of last week's spotlight — the leverage cue should now feel automatic.
- Lateral hinge AWAY from bell side
- Press up by moving body DOWN — bell barely moves
- Eye on bell, slow descent, controlled

**LOAD:** 25lb (max bell of the day). Light intent — drill the **left side** transitions (still the weak link).
- Pause every checkpoint: lying → elbow → hand → hip bridge → leg sweep → lunge → stand → reverse
- Reverse with the same discipline
- Left side first
60s each side. Hip flexor release — KB flow + cossack + lateral lunge fries the hip flexors. This counters.
60s. Adductors get tight from B-stance, cossack squats, and lateral lunges.

tips: **THURSDAY = SKILL + MOBILITY, NOT WORKOUT.** ~23 min, light. Recovery flank between Wed pull and Fri legs.
**Spotlight: windmill mechanics.** Drill the long-spine + back-leg hip-flexor reach at 18-20lb. The bent press's sibling lift — same hinge family.
**Zero leg loading.** No swings, no C&P, no goblet squats, no lunges.
Heaviest bell: 25lb (TGU only). Most of the session at 18-20lb.
**Banded prep diagonal reach is today's direct prime** — half-windmill with band sets up the windmill spotlight.
Should feel effortless. If anything feels like work, you've gone too hard.
Rotation plan: W12 TGU left-side checkpoints · W13 cycle back to bent press.
formPhilosophy: Thursday = skill becomes default movement. Windmill at light load drills the lateral hinge family deeper now that the bent press version is automatic. No load chase, no rep chase — the cue is the whole point. Hips and adductors get the cooldown they need.
